mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 100k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in INDPRO contraction periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC exceeds 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 900k signals strong construction activity that correlates with positive equity market performance through improved consumer confidence and corporate demand.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in HOUST > 900k periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC drops below 0.07.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in both the federal funds rate and CPI inflation signal accommodative policy and deflationary risks, creating a negative feedback loop that drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in co-contraction periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a weakening economic cycle, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in late-cycle regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 7.5% combined with declining payroll growth signals labor market deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in recessionary pressure regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in consumer credit availability above $425 billion correlates with heightened risk-taking in equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in RSAFS > 425000 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC drops below 0.06.
